codeReview.summary: The commit fixes the targeted generic `Self` regression paths and adds focused regression coverage, but it does so by weakening `types_compatible` globally. Those new compatibility rules accept invalid generic programs outside the `Self` substitution case, so the feature is not semantically sound.

issues.description: `types_compatible` now treats every `TParam` as compatible with every type in both directions. That means generic type parameters stop enforcing any constraint at all: e.g. the existing negative tests `invalid_generic_return` and `invalid_generic_assign` in `test/test_typecheck.ml` rely on `TParam` not matching arbitrary concrete types, but under this rule `TParam "T"` will accept `i32`, `str`, etc. The requested fix was to preserve concrete generic arguments during `Self` substitution, not to make generic parameters universal wildcards.

The new struct/enum compatibility rule accepts nominal generic types with matching names whenever either side has an empty argument list. That makes bare `Box` compatible with `Box<i32>`, `Box<str>`, etc. everywhere, not just in the specific bare-literal/associated-function `Self` substitution path this feature was meant to repair. Existing negative coverage such as `invalid_bare_vs_instantiated` in `test/test_typecheck.ml` shows this distinction matters.

summary: Reviewed the feature metadata, worker handoff/transcript skeleton, commit `6a0fb86`, and the affected typechecker/tests. The implementation does address the reported generic `Self` substitution regression and adds relevant regression tests, but it introduces globally unsound generic compatibility in `types_compatible`, so the review remains a fail.
